sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Withdraw treasury funds to build and launch Alchemy, a Cardano-native Bitcoin treasury/BTCfi infrastructure layer with FIRE and ICE assets, reserve architecture, dashboards, integrations, reporting, audits, administration, and staged launch liquidity.
Recipient: Sundial Protocol and Charms, with Intersect proposed as interim fund administrator subject to confirmation and final agreement

Deliverables: Shared BTC reserve architecture with transparent reserve-ratio mechanics and safety zones, Composable Cardano-native FIRE and ICE assets, Open-source SDKs and adapters for wallets, DEXs, and DeFi protocols, Always-on public dashboards for reserve ratio, supply, liquidity health, fee flows, and deployment status, Monthly governance reports and reporting standards, Independent security review, audit, and economic modeling, Three-month staged treasury-supported launch liquidity deployment, Legal, compliance, disclosure, treasury reporting, and launch-readiness work, Go-to-market, ecosystem education, DRep communications, partner activation, and launch coordination
Deadline/expiry: Three-month liquidity deployment after enactment with monthly reporting; no overall final delivery date stated beyond staged deployment and milestone conditions

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using an approximate USD 0.20 per ada reference rate for an approximately USD 2.0 million planning budget split into two approximately USD 1.0 million pools.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Pool 1 will support protocol infrastructure and staged launch liquidity, remain separate from operating expenses, deploy over three months, and return profits, yield, and returns generated by the launch liquidity position to the Cardano Treasury quarterly.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Pool 2 will fund delivery, audit, integrations, dashboards, governance reporting, legal and compliance work, and go-to-market execution with itemized planning allocations totaling approximately USD 1.0 million.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Alchemy will use a shared BTC reserve for FIRE and ICE with reserve-ratio formulas, a 5.0x initial target reserve ratio, and safety zones that constrain minting, redemption, and risky activity as reserve conditions weaken.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): FIRE is described as a residual, volatility-absorbing BTC exposure asset, while ICE is described as a lower-volatility USD-denominated BTC-backed exposure asset, and both are intended to be composable Cardano-native primitives.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal asserts that Cardano currently lacks a serious production BTCfi infrastructure layer and that failure to fund Alchemy would leave Bitcoin-backed structured exposure to consolidate on other chains and off-chain markets.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal asserts that Cardano currently lacks a serious production BTCfi infrastructure layer and that failure to fund Alchemy would leave Bitcoin-backed structured exposure to consolidate on other chains and off-chain markets.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using an approximate USD 0.20 per ada reference rate for an approximately USD 2.0 million planning budget split into two approximately USD 1.0 million pools." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal asserts that Cardano currently lacks a serious production BTCfi infrastructure layer and that failure to fund Alchemy would leave Bitcoin-backed structured exposure to consolidate on other chains and off-chain markets." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
